message:
rich_rust-cwe: validate Unicode Cell Width against RICH_SPEC Section 12

Added comprehensive spec validation tests covering:
- Basic width concept (ASCII=1, CJK=2, control=0)
- Cell width ranges (combining marks, Hangul Jamo, ideographic space)
- ASCII fast-path detection
- cell_len algorithm for pure/mixed content
- set_cell_size operations (exact fit, padding, truncation)
- chop_cells operations (ASCII, CJK, exact boundary, zero width)
- cell_positions mapping
- cell_to_byte_index conversion
- has_wide_chars detection
- Empty string edge cases
- Full-width punctuation

All 23 cells tests pass.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

 {"id":"rich_rust-0y1","title":"Add Thread Safety Verification","description":"## Objective
Verify and document thread safety of all public types.

## Background
Rust libraries should be clear about thread safety. Any global state (like caches) must be properly synchronized.

## Audit Areas

### Global State
- [ ] Color cache (if using global LRU cache) - needs Mutex
- [ ] Cell width cache (if using global) - needs Mutex or thread-local
- [ ] Any other lazy_static or OnceCell

### Type Thread Safety
For each public type, verify:
- [ ] Send + Sync if appropriate
- [ ] Documented thread safety guarantees
- [ ] No interior mutability surprises

### Specific Types to Verify
- [ ] Color: should be Send + Sync (no interior mutability)
- [ ] Style: should be Send + Sync
- [ ] Text: verify Send + Sync
- [ ] Segment: should be Send + Sync
- [ ] Console: may have interior mutability for recording
- [ ] Table, Panel, etc.: should be Send + Sync

### Caching Strategy
If using global caches:
```rust
// Option 1: Mutex (simple, some contention)
lazy_static! {
    static ref CACHE: Mutex<LruCache<String, Color>> = Mutex::new(LruCache::new(256));
}

// Option 2: Thread-local (no contention, per-thread memory)
thread_local! {
    static CACHE: RefCell<LruCache<String, Color>> = RefCell::new(LruCache::new(256));
}

// Option 3: dashmap (concurrent HashMap, best for high contention)
static CACHE: Lazy<DashMap<String, Color>> = Lazy::new(DashMap::new);
```

### Testing
```rust
#[test]
fn test_color_is_send_sync() {
    fn assert_send_sync<T: Send + Sync>() {}
    assert_send_sync::<Color>();
}

#[test]
fn test_concurrent_color_parsing() {
    let handles: Vec<_> = (0..10)
        .map(|_| {
            std::thread::spawn(|| {
                for _ in 0..1000 {
                    Color::parse("red").unwrap();
                    Color::parse("#ff0000").unwrap();
                }
            })
        })
        .collect();
    
    for h in handles {
        h.join().unwrap();
    }
}
```

## Deliverables
1. Thread safety audit document
2. Tests verifying Send + Sync
3. Documentation of any thread-safety caveats","acceptance_criteria":"All types audited for thread safety. Documentation updated. Concurrent tests pass.","status":"open","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","owner":"jeff141421@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-01-17T10:47:49.199193872-05:00","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone","updated_at":"2026-01-17T10:47:49.199193872-05:00","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"rich_rust-0y1","depends_on_id":"rich_rust-6os","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-17T10:48:04.438088602-05:00","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone"},{"issue_id":"rich_rust-0y1","depends_on_id":"rich_rust-8ut","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-17T10:48:04.480950632-05:00","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone"}]}
 {"id":"rich_rust-10p","title":"Verify and Fix Text.divide() Algorithm","description":"## Objective
Ensure Text.divide() implementation matches RICH_SPEC.md Section 6 exactly.

## Background
Text.divide() splits styled text at specified character offsets. This is critical for table cell rendering where content must be split at column boundaries. Incorrect splitting can corrupt styles.

## Technical Details

### Spec Requirement (Section 6.6)
divide(offsets) splits text at character positions, correctly handling:
- Span boundaries that don't align with offsets
- Partial spans that cross division points
- Multiple overlapping spans

### Example
```
Text: 'hello world!' with span on 'world' (chars 6-11)
divide([5, 10]) should produce:
  - [0-5]: 'hello' (no style)
  - [5-10]: ' worl' (partial 'world' style on 'worl')
  - [10-12]: 'd!' (partial 'world' style on 'd')
```

### Verification Steps
1. Read current Text.divide() implementation
2. Write test cases matching spec examples
3. Compare behavior to Python Rich's Text.divide()
4. Fix any discrepancies

### Critical Edge Cases
- Span starts exactly at division point
- Span ends exactly at division point
- Multiple spans crossing same division
- Empty divisions (adjacent offsets)
- Wide characters at division boundary

## Files to Examine
- src/text.rs (divide method)
- Python Rich text.py (reference)
- RICH_SPEC.md Section 6.6","acceptance_criteria":"Text.divide() matches spec exactly. All edge cases pass. Test coverage complete.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","owner":"jeff141421@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-01-17T10:20:53.299677283-05:00","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone","updated_at":"2026-01-17T23:39:22.062442797-05:00","closed_at":"2026-01-17T23:39:22.062442797-05:00","close_reason":"Validated: Text.divide() implementation at text.rs:417-439 matches RICH_SPEC Section 6.4.

Uses slice() method which correctly handles:
- Span boundaries that don't align with offsets (intersection calculation)
- Partial spans that cross division points (proper coordinate adjustment)
- Multiple overlapping spans (processed in order)

Example verified: 'hello world!' with span on 'world' (chars 6-11), divide([5,10]) correctly produces:
- 'hello' (no style)
- ' worl' (span adjusted to [1,5])
- 'd!' (span adjusted to [0,1])

Test test_text_divide passes. Implementation functionally equivalent to spec algorithm.","labels":["critical","implementation"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"rich_rust-10p","depends_on_id":"rich_rust-a96","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-17T10:22:53.572450463-05:00","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone"},{"issue_id":"rich_rust-10p","depends_on_id":"rich_rust-4sr","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-17T10:43:22.807068406-05:00","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone"}]}
 {"id":"rich_rust-1ht","title":"Add Style ANSI Code Caching","description":"## Objective
Cache rendered ANSI codes for frequently-used styles.

## Background
Styles are often reused (bold, colors like red/green). Computing ANSI codes repeatedly is wasteful. Cache the rendered ANSI for common styles.

## Implementation Approach

### Option 1: LazyCell per Style
Store rendered ANSI lazily in Style struct.
Downside: Increases Style size, may not be Clone-friendly.

### Option 2: Global LRU Cache
Similar to color parsing cache.
Key: Style (need Hash impl)
Value: Rendered ANSI string

```rust
lazy_static! {
    static ref ANSI_CACHE: Mutex<LruCache<Style, String>> = 
        Mutex::new(LruCache::new(256));
}
```

### Option 3: Thread-Local Cache
Avoid mutex overhead with thread-local storage.

```rust
thread_local! {
    static ANSI_CACHE: RefCell<LruCache<Style, String>> = 
        RefCell::new(LruCache::new(256));
}
```

## Considerations
- Style must implement Hash and Eq
- Cache size limits
- Thread safety vs performance tradeoff
- Benchmark to verify improvement

## Files to Modify
- src/style.rs

## Dependencies
- Style needs Hash implementation
- Consider after other caching tasks","acceptance_criteria":"ANSI caching implemented. Benchmarks show improvement for repeated styles.","status":"open","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","owner":"jeff141421@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-01-17T10:24:57.492383823-05:00","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone","updated_at":"2026-01-17T10:24:57.492383823-05:00","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"rich_rust-1ht","depends_on_id":"rich_rust-yz6","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-17T10:25:06.934209961-05:00","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone"},{"issue_id":"rich_rust-1ht","depends_on_id":"rich_rust-wph","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-17T10:43:24.294281454-05:00","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone"}]}
-{"id":"rich_rust-1qo","title":"Create E2E Test: Performance Regression Suite","description":"## Objective
E2E tests that establish and monitor performance baselines.

## Background
Performance regressions can creep in gradually. These tests establish baselines and fail if performance degrades significantly.

## Test Scenarios

### Scenario 1: Large Table Rendering Time
```rust
#[test]
fn test_perf_large_table() {
    let _guard = init_test_logging();
    
    let table = create_large_table(100, 10);  // 100 rows, 10 columns
    let console = Console::new().width(200);
    
    let start = Instant::now();
    let _output = console.render_to_string(&table);
    let elapsed = start.elapsed();
    
    info!(
        rows = 100,
        columns = 10,
        elapsed_ms = elapsed.as_millis(),
        "Large table render time"
    );
    
    // Baseline: should complete in under 100ms
    assert!(
        elapsed < Duration::from_millis(100),
        "Table rendering too slow: {:?}", elapsed
    );
}
```

### Scenario 2: Color Parsing Performance
- Parse 10,000 colors
- Verify: Under 50ms with caching

### Scenario 3: Text Wrapping Performance
- Wrap 10,000 character text
- Verify: Under 20ms

### Scenario 4: Markup Parsing Performance
- Parse complex nested markup
- Verify: Linear scaling

### Scenario 5: Memory Usage
- Render large document
- Verify: Memory stays bounded

## Baseline Management
```rust
// tests/perf_baselines.json
{
    "large_table_100x10_ms": 100,
    "color_parse_10000_ms": 50,
    "text_wrap_10000_chars_ms": 20,
    "markup_parse_complex_ms": 10
}
```

## Logging Requirements
- Timing for each phase
- Memory usage snapshots
- Comparison to baseline
- Percentage change from baseline

## CI Integration
- Store baselines in repo
- Update baselines with `UPDATE_PERF_BASELINE=1`
- Fail on >20% regression

## Files to Create
- tests/e2e_performance.rs
- tests/perf_baselines.json","acceptance_criteria":"Performance baselines established. Regressions detected automatically.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","owner":"jeff141421@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-01-17T10:45:05.009350381-05:00","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone","updated_at":"2026-01-17T10:45:05.009350381-05:00","labels":["e2e","performance","testing"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"rich_rust-1qo","depends_on_id":"rich_rust-6d6","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-17T10:45:13.01541569-05:00","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone"}]}

 {"id":"rich_rust-26r","title":"Verify Table Width Expand Algorithm","description":"## Objective
Verify expand_widths() in Table matches RICH_SPEC.md Section 9 exactly.

## Background
When table has extra space available, expand_widths() distributes it among columns based on their ratios. This uses the ratio distribution algorithm.

## Technical Details

### Spec Requirement (Section 9.4)
expand_widths() should:
1. Calculate excess = available_width - current_total
2. Distribute extra space using ratio_distribute()
3. Columns without explicit ratio get default ratio of 1
4. Columns with ratio=0 get no extra space

### Algorithm Verification
```
Given: 3 columns with widths [20, 20, 20] = 60
       ratios [1, 2, 1] (second column gets 2x share)
       available width = 100
       excess = 40

Step 1: Calculate ratio distribution
  total_ratio = 1 + 2 + 1 = 4
  col0 gets: 40 * (1/4) = 10
  col1 gets: 40 * (2/4) = 20
  col2 gets: 40 * (1/4) = 10

Result: [30, 40, 30]
```

### Test Cases
1. Equal ratios - equal distribution
2. Different ratios - proportional distribution
3. Zero ratio column - gets no extra space
4. Single column - gets all extra
5. Rounding edge cases

## Files to Examine
- src/renderables/table.rs (expand_widths function)
- RICH_SPEC.md Section 9.4, Section 14","acceptance_criteria":"expand_widths uses ratio_distribute correctly. All test cases pass.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","owner":"jeff141421@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-01-17T10:22:10.385823223-05:00","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone","updated_at":"2026-01-17T23:36:54.908252436-05:00","closed_at":"2026-01-17T23:36:54.908252436-05:00","close_reason":"Validated: expand_widths() matches RICH_SPEC Section 14.4 exactly. Tests pass:
- test_expand_widths_with_ratios: widths [20,20,20] + ratios [1,2,1] = [30,40,30] ✓
- test_expand_widths_minimal_ratio_case: ratio 1:3 distribution works ✓
Implementation uses Ratio<usize> for exact fractional arithmetic. See table.rs:700-747.","labels":["critical","implementation"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"rich_rust-26r","depends_on_id":"rich_rust-a96","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-17T10:23:03.835304272-05:00","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone"},{"issue_id":"rich_rust-26r","depends_on_id":"rich_rust-q5f","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-17T10:46:58.139024109-05:00","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone"}]}
 {"id":"rich_rust-2ah","title":"Verify API Ergonomics and Rust Idioms","description":"Review and improve API ergonomics to ensure idiomatic Rust usage.

PURPOSE:
Ensure rich_rust provides a pleasant, idiomatic Rust API that follows community conventions and leverages Rust type system effectively.

ERGONOMICS CHECKLIST:

1. BUILDER PATTERNS
- [ ] All complex structs use builder pattern
- [ ] Builders are chainable (return Self)
- [ ] Builders have sensible defaults
- [ ] Terminal build() method returns owned type
- [ ] Example: Table::new().title("X").add_row(...).build()

2. FROM IMPLEMENTATIONS
- [ ] From<&str> for Text
- [ ] From<String> for Text
- [ ] From<&str> for Color (if infallible)
- [ ] TryFrom<&str> for Color (if fallible)
- [ ] From<(u8, u8, u8)> for ColorTriplet
- [ ] Into<Style> for common types

3. DEFAULT IMPLEMENTATIONS
- [ ] All public structs with sensible defaults impl Default
- [ ] Default creates useful zero-config instances
- [ ] Example: Console::default() works reasonably

4. DISPLAY IMPLEMENTATIONS
- [ ] Debug for all public types
- [ ] Display where semantic (Color, Style descriptions)
- [ ] Error types impl std::error::Error

5. CLONE/COPY SEMANTICS
- [ ] Small value types are Copy (ColorTriplet)
- [ ] Larger types are Clone
- [ ] Consider Cow<str> for string-heavy types

6. GENERIC PARAMETERS
- [ ] Functions accept impl AsRef<str> where appropriate
- [ ] Use Into<T> for owned conversions
- [ ] Avoid overly generic signatures that hurt error messages

7. ERROR HANDLING
- [ ] Custom error types with helpful messages
- [ ] Errors implement std::error::Error
- [ ] Consider anyhow/thiserror patterns

8. ITERATORS
- [ ] Types that contain collections expose iterators
- [ ] IntoIterator implementations
- [ ] Iterator adapters where useful

FILES TO REVIEW:
- All public API surfaces in src/*.rs
- src/prelude.rs exports

ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA:
- API feels natural to Rust developers
- No clippy warnings on API usage
- Documentation examples compile cleanly","status":"in_progress","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","owner":"jeff141421@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-01-17T11:14:25.444405212-05:00","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone","updated_at":"2026-01-17T17:54:00.704337889-05:00"}
 {"id":"rich_rust-2oy","title":"EPIC: Documentation & Examples","description":"## Objective
Create comprehensive documentation and examples for rich_rust users.

## Background
Good documentation is essential for adoption. This includes:
1. API documentation (rustdoc)
2. User guide with examples
3. Migration guide from Python Rich
4. Example programs

## Scope

### API Documentation
- Module-level documentation
- Struct/function documentation
- Code examples in doc comments

### User Guide
- Getting started
- Core concepts (Console, Style, Text, Segment)
- Renderables (Table, Panel, Tree, etc.)
- Advanced topics

### Examples
- Basic usage
- Tables
- Panels
- Progress bars
- Custom renderables

### Migration Guide
- Python Rich -> rich_rust mapping
- Feature parity table
- Known differences

## Success Criteria
- All public APIs documented
- Examples compile and run
- User can learn from docs without source code","acceptance_criteria":"All public APIs documented. Examples work. Migration guide complete.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"epic","owner":"jeff141421@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-01-17T10:25:09.333195865-05:00","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone","updated_at":"2026-01-17T10:25:09.333195865-05:00"}

 {"id":"rich_rust-4s3","title":"Validate Measurement Protocol against RICH_SPEC Section 5","description":"## Objective
Verify the Measurement implementation in src/measure.rs matches RICH_SPEC.md Section 5 exactly.

## Background
The Measurement protocol determines how much horizontal space a renderable needs. It returns minimum and maximum widths, enabling the layout system to calculate optimal column widths for tables and panels.

## Validation Checklist

### 5.1 Measurement Struct
- [ ] Verify fields: minimum (usize), maximum (usize)
- [ ] Verify invariant: minimum <= maximum always maintained
- [ ] Verify Measurement::new(minimum, maximum) constructor
- [ ] Verify constructor normalizes if minimum > maximum

### 5.2 Measurement Methods
- [ ] Verify normalize() ensures minimum <= maximum
- [ ] Verify with_maximum(width) clamps both values
- [ ] Verify with_minimum(width) sets floor for both values
- [ ] Verify clamp(min, max) constrains to range
- [ ] Verify union(other) takes max of maximums, min of minimums
- [ ] Verify span accessor returns maximum - minimum

### 5.3 Utility Functions
- [ ] Verify measure_union(measurements) unions all measurements
- [ ] Verify measure_sum(measurements) sums all measurements
- [ ] Verify get_measurement returns Measurement for any Measurable

### 5.4 Measurable Trait (if implemented)
- [ ] Verify trait signature: fn measure(&self, max_width: usize) -> Measurement
- [ ] Verify implementations for: Text, Segment, Table, Panel

### 5.5 Edge Cases
- [ ] Test measurement with zero width content
- [ ] Test measurement with very large content
- [ ] Test clamp with inverted min/max

## Files to Examine
- src/measure.rs (primary)
- RICH_SPEC.md Section 5","acceptance_criteria":"All checklist items verified. Invariant maintenance confirmed.","notes":"Validation complete: Measurement struct, Measurement::get, and measure_renderables match RICH_SPEC Section 5; no code changes needed. Blocked only by epic rich_rust-q1k closure.","status":"blocked","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","owner":"jeff141421@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-01-17T10:17:57.545908076-05:00","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone","updated_at":"2026-01-17T21:44:10.464454021-05:00","labels":["phase-1","validation"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"rich_rust-4s3","depends_on_id":"rich_rust-q1k","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-17T10:19:35.860321866-05:00","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone"}]}
 {"id":"rich_rust-4sr","title":"Validate Text Operations against RICH_SPEC Section 6","description":"## Objective
Verify the Text implementation in src/text.rs matches RICH_SPEC.md Section 6 exactly.

## Background
Text is the primary user-facing type for styled text. It supports overlapping style spans, wrapping, justification, and conversion to segments. This is one of the most complex modules.

## Validation Checklist

### 6.1 Text Struct
- [ ] Verify fields: plain (String), spans (Vec<Span>), style (Option<Style>), justify (JustifyMethod), overflow (OverflowMethod), tab_size (usize), no_wrap (bool)
- [ ] Verify Span struct: start (usize), end (usize), style (Style)
- [ ] Verify JustifyMethod enum: Default, Left, Center, Right, Full
- [ ] Verify OverflowMethod enum: Fold, Crop, Ellipsis

### 6.2 Text Construction
- [ ] Verify Text::new() creates empty text
- [ ] Verify Text::plain(text) creates unstyled text
- [ ] Verify Text::styled(text, style) creates styled text
- [ ] Verify From<String> and From<&str> implementations

### 6.3 Text Manipulation
- [ ] Verify append(text) adds plain text
- [ ] Verify append_styled(text, style) adds styled text
- [ ] Verify stylize(start, end, style) applies span
- [ ] Verify highlight_words(words, style) highlights matches
- [ ] Verify highlight_regex(pattern, style) highlights regex matches

### 6.4 Text Rendering
- [ ] Verify render() returns Vec<Segment>
- [ ] Verify spans are applied in order with proper overlap handling
- [ ] Verify base style is applied before spans
- [ ] Verify tab characters are expanded to tab_size spaces

### 6.5 Text Wrapping (CRITICAL - complex algorithm)
- [ ] Verify wrap(width) returns Vec<Text> lines
- [ ] Verify word boundaries are respected
- [ ] Verify long words are broken at width
- [ ] Verify overflow methods: Fold (break anywhere), Crop (truncate), Ellipsis (truncate with ...)
- [ ] Verify no_wrap flag prevents wrapping

### 6.6 Text.divide() Algorithm (CRITICAL)
- [ ] Verify divide(offsets) splits text at character positions
- [ ] Verify spans are correctly split across divisions
- [ ] Verify partial spans are properly trimmed
- [ ] Test: divide([5,10]) on 'hello world!' with span on 'world'

### 6.7 Justification
- [ ] Verify left justify (no change)
- [ ] Verify right justify (pad left)
- [ ] Verify center justify (pad both sides)
- [ ] Verify full justify (expand spaces between words)

## Files to Examine
- src/text.rs (primary)
- RICH_SPEC.md Section 6","acceptance_criteria":"All checklist items verified. Text.divide() algorithm matches spec exactly. Wrapping edge cases handled.","status":"in_progress","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","owner":"jeff141421@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-01-17T10:17:58.566928519-05:00","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone","updated_at":"2026-01-17T23:41:13.430302678-05:00","labels":["phase-1","validation"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"rich_rust-4sr","depends_on_id":"rich_rust-q1k","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-17T10:19:36.985802031-05:00","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone"}]}
 {"id":"rich_rust-5vo","title":"Validate Panel against RICH_SPEC Section 10","description":"## Objective
Verify the Panel implementation in src/renderables/panel.rs matches RICH_SPEC.md Section 10 exactly.

## Background
Panel draws a bordered box around content with optional title and subtitle. It uses the Box system for borders and supports content alignment.

## Validation Checklist

### 10.1 Panel Struct
- [ ] Verify fields: content, title, subtitle, box_style, border_style, title_align, subtitle_align, padding, expand, width, height, highlight, safe_box

### 10.2 Panel Construction
- [ ] Verify Panel::new(content) constructor
- [ ] Verify builder methods: title(), subtitle(), box_style(), etc.
- [ ] Verify Panel::fit(content) creates minimal-width panel

### 10.3 Panel Rendering
- [ ] Verify top border with title integration
- [ ] Verify content area with padding
- [ ] Verify bottom border with subtitle integration
- [ ] Verify title alignment: left, center, right

### 10.4 Panel Sizing
- [ ] Verify width calculation includes borders and padding
- [ ] Verify expand=true fills available width
- [ ] Verify explicit width overrides
- [ ] Verify height expands to fit content

### 10.5 Integration
- [ ] Test Panel containing Text
- [ ] Test Panel containing Table
- [ ] Test nested Panels

## Files to Examine
- src/renderables/panel.rs (primary)
- src/box.rs (BoxChars)

| subtitle | Option<Text> | Option<Text> | ✅ |
| subtitle_align | JustifyMethod | JustifyMethod | ✅ |

---

### 10.4 Rendering Structure - ALIGNED

| Step | Spec | Impl | Status |
|------|------|------|--------|
| Safe box handling | ✅ | effective_box() | ✅ |
| Width calculation | expand/width/measure | expand/width/content_width() | ✅ |
| Top border + title | insert_title_into_border | render_top_border() | ✅ |
| Top padding | loop with blank lines | loop with spaces | ✅ |
| Content with borders | head[0] + content + head[3] | Same | ✅ |
| Bottom padding | loop with blank lines | loop with spaces | ✅ |
| Bottom border + subtitle | Similar to top | render_bottom_border() | ✅ |

---

### Constructors & Methods - ALIGNED

- Panel::new(content_lines) ✅
- Panel::from_text(str) ✅
- Panel::from_rich_text(text, width) ✅
- fit_panel(text) ✅ (global function)
- Builder methods: title(), subtitle(), box_style(), padding(), etc. ✅

---

### Key Differences

1. **Content Storage**: Impl stores pre-rendered Vec<Vec<Segment>> vs spec's Box<dyn Renderable>. This simplifies rendering but prevents dynamic re-layout.

2. **highlight field**: Not implemented (used for background highlighting).

3. **fit()**: Implemented as global fit_panel() function.

 {"id":"rich_rust-62c","title":"Create E2E Test: Complete Rendering Pipeline","description":"## Objective
Create end-to-end tests that verify the complete rendering pipeline from markup to ANSI output.

## Background
The rendering pipeline is: markup string → parse → styled segments → ANSI codes → terminal output. E2E tests verify this entire chain works correctly.

## Test Scenarios

### Scenario 1: Basic Markup Rendering
```rust
#[test]
fn test_e2e_basic_markup() {
    let _guard = init_test_logging();
    info\!("Starting E2E basic markup test");
    
    let console = Console::new().width(80).color_system(ColorSystem::TrueColor);
    let mut output = Vec::new();
    
    info\!(input = "[bold red]Hello[/] [green]World[/]\!", "Rendering markup");
    console.render_to(&mut output, "[bold red]Hello[/] [green]World[/]\!");
    
    let result = String::from_utf8(output).unwrap();
    debug\!(output = %result, "Raw ANSI output");
    
    // Verify ANSI codes present
    assert\!(result.contains("\x1b[1m"), "Missing bold code");  // bold
    assert\!(result.contains("\x1b[31m") || result.contains("\x1b[38;2;"), "Missing red");
    assert\!(result.contains("Hello"), "Missing text content");
    
    info\!("E2E basic markup test PASSED");
}
```

### Scenario 2: Nested Styles
- Test: `[bold][red][underline]text[/][/][/]`
- Verify: All three styles applied, proper reset sequence

### Scenario 3: Style Override
- Test: `[red]a[blue]b[/]c[/]`
- Verify: 'a' is red, 'b' is blue, 'c' is red again

### Scenario 4: Color System Downgrade
- Test same markup with TrueColor, EightBit, Standard
- Verify: Output is valid for each system

### Scenario 5: Wide Characters
- Test: `[bold]你好世界[/]`
- Verify: Correct cell width calculation

### Scenario 6: Hyperlinks
- Test: `[link=https://example.com]Click[/]`
- Verify: OSC 8 sequences present

## Logging Requirements
Each test must log:
1. Test name and scenario at INFO level
2. Input data at DEBUG level
3. Intermediate state (parsed segments) at TRACE level
4. Output data at DEBUG level
5. Assertion results at INFO level
6. Timing for each phase

## Golden File Comparison
```rust
fn assert_golden(name: &str, actual: &str) {
    let golden_path = format\!("tests/golden/{}.txt", name);
    if std::env::var("UPDATE_GOLDEN").is_ok() {
        std::fs::write(&golden_path, actual).unwrap();
        info\!(path = %golden_path, "Updated golden file");
    } else {
        let expected = std::fs::read_to_string(&golden_path).unwrap();
        if actual \!= expected {
            error\!(expected = %expected, actual = %actual, "Golden file mismatch");
            panic\!("Output doesn't match golden file");
        }
        info\!("Golden file comparison PASSED");
    }
}
```

## Files to Create
- tests/e2e_rendering.rs
- tests/golden/*.txt (golden files)","acceptance_criteria":"All pipeline scenarios pass. Golden files established. Logs show full trace.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","owner":"jeff141421@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-01-17T10:44:28.537406796-05:00","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone","updated_at":"2026-01-17T12:25:03.535019652-05:00","closed_at":"2026-01-17T12:25:03.535019652-05:00","close_reason":"E2E rendering pipeline tests complete: created tests/e2e_rendering.rs with 18 tests (plus 8 common test utilities) covering basic markup (3), nested styles (2), style override (1), color system downgrade (3), wide characters (2), hyperlinks (1), edge cases (3), and snapshot tests (2). All 26 tests pass.","labels":["e2e","testing"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"rich_rust-62c","depends_on_id":"rich_rust-6d6","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-17T10:45:12.834667593-05:00","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone"},{"issue_id":"rich_rust-62c","depends_on_id":"rich_rust-4ot","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-17T11:11:53.963675025-05:00","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone"}]}
 {"id":"rich_rust-6d6","title":"Set Up Test Logging Infrastructure with Tracing","description":"## Objective
Establish comprehensive logging infrastructure for tests using the `tracing` crate.

## Background
Detailed logging is essential for debugging test failures, especially in CI environments where you can't interactively debug. The `tracing` crate provides structured, leveled logging that integrates well with Rust's async ecosystem and testing frameworks.

## Implementation Requirements

### Dependencies to Add (Cargo.toml)
```toml
[dev-dependencies]
tracing = "0.1"
tracing-subscriber = { version = "0.3", features = ["env-filter", "fmt", "json"] }
tracing-test = "0.2"
test-log = { version = "0.2", features = ["trace"] }
```

### Test Logging Setup
```rust
// tests/common/mod.rs
use tracing_subscriber::{fmt, prelude::*, EnvFilter};

pub fn init_test_logging() {
    let _ = tracing_subscriber::registry()
        .with(fmt::layer()
            .with_test_writer()
            .with_ansi(true)
            .with_file(true)
            .with_line_number(true)
            .with_thread_ids(true)
            .with_target(true))
        .with(EnvFilter::from_default_env()
            .add_directive("rich_rust=debug".parse().unwrap()))
        .try_init();
}
```

### Logging Conventions
1. **Function entry/exit**: Use `#[instrument]` attribute on key functions
2. **Decision points**: Log at DEBUG level with context
3. **Errors**: Log at ERROR level with full context
4. **Performance**: Log timing spans with `tracing::info_span!`
5. **Test phases**: Clear markers for setup/execution/assertion

### Log Output Format
```
2026-01-17T12:34:56.789Z DEBUG rich_rust::color [thread:1] parse: input="#ff0000"
2026-01-17T12:34:56.790Z DEBUG rich_rust::color [thread:1] parse: result=TrueColor(255,0,0)
2026-01-17T12:34:56.791Z  INFO test_color_parsing: assertion passed, expected=#ff0000, actual=#ff0000
```

### Environment Variables
- `RUST_LOG=debug` - Enable debug logging in tests
- `RUST_LOG=rich_rust::color=trace` - Module-specific tracing
- `TEST_LOG_JSON=1` - Output JSON format for CI parsing

## Files to Create
- tests/common/mod.rs (logging setup)
- tests/common/assertions.rs (logged assertion helpers)

## Verification
- Run `RUST_LOG=debug cargo test -- --nocapture`
- Verify structured output
- Verify spans show timing","acceptance_criteria":"Logging infrastructure works. Tests produce detailed, structured logs.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","owner":"jeff141421@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-01-17T10:44:27.209355873-05:00","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone","updated_at":"2026-01-17T11:38:41.476872412-05:00","closed_at":"2026-01-17T11:38:41.476872412-05:00","close_reason":"Test logging infrastructure implemented with tracing. Added dependencies, created tests/common/mod.rs with init_test_logging(), test_phase(), and log_test_context(). Created tests/common/assertions.rs with 10 logged assertion helpers. All 14 tests pass. Future E2E tests can now use this infrastructure.","labels":["infrastructure","testing"]}
 {"id":"rich_rust-6dt","title":"Optimize String Allocation in Rendering","description":"## Objective
Reduce string allocations in the hot rendering path.

## Background
Rendering produces many small strings that are immediately discarded. This creates GC pressure and cache misses. Optimizations include buffer reuse and preallocated capacity.

## Areas to Optimize

### ANSI Code Generation
Current: Each style.render_to_ansi() allocates new String
Optimization: Pass mutable buffer to append to

```rust
// Before
fn render_to_ansi(&self) -> String {
    let mut result = String::new();
    // ...
    result
}

// After
fn render_to_ansi_into(&self, buf: &mut String) {
    buf.clear();
    // ...append directly...
}
```

### Segment Collection
Current: Vec<Segment> allocated for each renderable
Optimization: Use SmallVec or reuse buffers

### Text Building
Current: Many String concatenations
Optimization: Use String::with_capacity() with estimated size

### Console Output
Current: Build complete output then write
Optimization: Write directly to BufWriter

## Measurement
- Before/after benchmarks for each change
- Memory profiling (heaptrack or similar)

## Files to Modify
- src/style.rs
- src/segment.rs
- src/console.rs
- Cargo.toml (add smallvec if needed)","acceptance_criteria":"Measurable reduction in allocations. Benchmarks improved.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","owner":"jeff141421@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-01-17T10:24:56.63731772-05:00","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone","updated_at":"2026-01-17T10:24:56.63731772-05:00","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"rich_rust-6dt","depends_on_id":"rich_rust-yz6","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-17T10:25:06.882092527-05:00","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone"},{"issue_id":"rich_rust-6dt","depends_on_id":"rich_rust-wph","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-17T10:43:24.25021802-05:00","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone"}]}
 {"id":"rich_rust-6os","title":"Implement LRU Cache for Color Parsing","description":"## Objective
Add LRU caching to color parsing as specified in RICH_SPEC.md Section 1.

## Background
Color parsing (especially named colors and hex parsing) is called frequently during rendering. The spec requires an LRU cache to avoid repeated parsing of the same color strings.

## Technical Details

### Current State
- Color::parse() parses color strings on every call
- No caching mechanism exists
- Repeated renders of same markup parse same colors repeatedly

### Required Implementation
1. Add LRU cache (suggest using lru crate or std HashMap with eviction)
2. Cache key: color string (e.g., 'red', '#ff0000', 'rgb(255,0,0)')
3. Cache value: parsed Color enum variant
4. Cache size: configurable, default 256 entries

### Implementation Approach
```rust
use std::sync::Mutex;
use lru::LruCache;

lazy_static! {
    static ref COLOR_CACHE: Mutex<LruCache<String, Color>> = 
        Mutex::new(LruCache::new(256));
}

pub fn parse_cached(s: &str) -> Result<Color, ColorError> {
    let mut cache = COLOR_CACHE.lock().unwrap();
    if let Some(color) = cache.get(s) {
        return Ok(color.clone());
    }
    let color = Color::parse(s)?;
    cache.put(s.to_string(), color.clone());
    Ok(color)
}
```

### Considerations
- Thread safety: Mutex or RwLock for concurrent access
- Memory: Limit cache size to prevent unbounded growth
- Performance: Benchmark before/after to verify improvement
- API: Decide if parse() always uses cache or separate parse_cached()

## Files to Modify
- src/color.rs (add caching)
- Cargo.toml (add lru dependency if needed)

## Testing
- Benchmark color parsing with/without cache
- Verify cache eviction works correctly
- Test thread safety with concurrent parsing","acceptance_criteria":"LRU cache implemented. Benchmarks show performance improvement. Thread-safe.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","owner":"jeff141421@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-01-17T10:20:41.980918356-05:00","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone","updated_at":"2026-01-17T10:20:41.980918356-05:00","labels":["implementation","performance"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"rich_rust-6os","depends_on_id":"rich_rust-a96","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-17T10:22:52.303872769-05:00","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone"},{"issue_id":"rich_rust-6os","depends_on_id":"rich_rust-yz6","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-17T10:25:08.549524038-05:00","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone"},{"issue_id":"rich_rust-6os","depends_on_id":"rich_rust-g92","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-17T10:43:22.740309401-05:00","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone"}]}
-{"id":"rich_rust-79n","title":"Validate Table against RICH_SPEC Section 9","description":"## Objective
Verify the Table implementation in src/renderables/table.rs matches RICH_SPEC.md Section 9 exactly.

## Background
Table is one of the most complex renderables. It handles column width calculation, row rendering with vertical alignment, borders, padding, and caption. The width algorithm is particularly critical.

## Validation Checklist

### 9.1 Table Struct
- [ ] Verify fields: columns, rows, title, caption, box_style, border_style, header_style, row_styles, padding, collapse_padding, show_header, show_edge, show_lines, expand

### 9.2 Column Struct
- [ ] Verify fields: header, footer, style, justify, vertical, width, min_width, max_width, ratio, no_wrap, overflow

### 9.3 Row/Cell Structs
- [ ] Verify Row contains Vec<Cell>
- [ ] Verify Cell: content, style, vertical_align, end_section

### 9.4 Width Calculation Algorithm (CRITICAL)
- [ ] Verify get_row_widths() measures each cell
- [ ] Verify calculate_widths() implements the algorithm:
  1. Start with minimum widths
  2. If total < available, expand_widths()
  3. If total > available, collapse_widths()
- [ ] Verify expand_widths() distributes extra space by ratio
- [ ] Verify collapse_widths() reduces widths proportionally
- [ ] Test: 3 columns [10,20,30] in 50-width table

### 9.5 Row Rendering
- [ ] Verify render_row() produces correct segments
- [ ] Verify vertical alignment: top, middle, bottom
- [ ] Verify padding is applied
- [ ] Verify borders are drawn correctly
- [ ] Verify multi-line cells are handled

### 9.6 Table Rendering
- [ ] Verify title appears above table
- [ ] Verify header row has header_style
- [ ] Verify row separators when show_lines=true
- [ ] Verify caption appears below table
- [ ] Verify expand=true fills available width

### 9.7 Edge Cases
- [ ] Test empty table
- [ ] Test single column
- [ ] Test very narrow width
- [ ] Test cells with wide characters (CJK)

## Files to Examine
- src/renderables/table.rs (primary)
- src/measure.rs (Measurement)
- RICH_SPEC.md Section 9","acceptance_criteria":"Width calculation algorithm verified. All table features work correctly.","notes":"## Split Into Focused Tasks

2. **rich_rust-q5f**: Validate Table Width Algorithms (Section 9.4) - CRITICAL
3. **rich_rust-0sw**: Validate Table Rendering (Section 9.5-9.6)

Complete those tasks to fulfill this validation.

## Rationale
The Table module is 1090 lines with complex algorithms. Breaking it into focused tasks allows:
- Parallel work on different aspects
- Clearer ownership and tracking
- Easier to identify specific issues","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","owner":"jeff141421@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-01-17T10:18:37.916962178-05:00","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone","updated_at":"2026-01-17T10:46:56.472385145-05:00","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"rich_rust-79n","depends_on_id":"rich_rust-q1k","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-17T10:19:37.140442934-05:00","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone"}]}

 {"id":"rich_rust-85g","title":"Verify Hyperlink (OSC 8) Support","description":"## Objective
Verify and complete hyperlink support using OSC 8 escape sequences.

## Background
Modern terminals support clickable hyperlinks via OSC 8 escape sequences. The Style struct has a link field, but rendering may be incomplete.

## Technical Details

### OSC 8 Format
```
ESC ] 8 ; params ; URI ST ... text ... ESC ] 8 ; ; ST
```
Where:
- ESC ] = 0x1B 0x5D (OSC introducer)
- ST = 0x1B 0x5C (string terminator) or 0x07 (BEL)
- params = optional key=value pairs (e.g., id=unique-id)
- URI = the link URL

### Example
```
\x1b]8;;https://example.com\x07Click here\x1b]8;;\x07
```

### Verification Checklist
- [ ] Style::link(url) stores URL correctly
- [ ] Style parsing handles 'link=URL' syntax
- [ ] ANSI rendering produces correct OSC 8 sequence
- [ ] Links work in markup: [link=URL]text[/]
- [ ] Nested styles with links render correctly
- [ ] Terminal compatibility: iTerm2, kitty, Windows Terminal

### Implementation Gaps (if found)
- May need to add OSC 8 rendering to style.rs render_to_ansi()
- May need console.rs to check terminal hyperlink support

## Files to Examine
- src/style.rs (link field, ANSI rendering)
- src/console.rs (terminal capability detection)
- RICH_SPEC.md Section 2.2","acceptance_criteria":"Hyperlinks render correctly. Tested in compatible terminals.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","owner":"jeff141421@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-01-17T10:21:03.561080644-05:00","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone","updated_at":"2026-01-17T10:21:03.561080644-05:00","labels":["implementation"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"rich_rust-85g","depends_on_id":"rich_rust-a96","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-17T10:22:54.797298549-05:00","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone"},{"issue_id":"rich_rust-85g","depends_on_id":"rich_rust-kko","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-17T10:43:23.03192436-05:00","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone"}]}

## Files to Create/Modify
- src/style.rs (add tests section)","acceptance_criteria":"100% test coverage for style.rs. Combination semantics verified.","notes":"## Logging Requirements

All tests must use the tracing infrastructure:
1. Call init_test_logging() at test start
2. Log style inputs at DEBUG level
3. Log combination results at DEBUG level
4. Log ANSI output at TRACE level (can be verbose)

Example:
```rust
#[test]
fn test_style_combine() {
    let _guard = init_test_logging();
    
    let bold = Style::new().bold();
    let red = Style::new().color(Color::Red);
    
    debug!(style1 = ?bold, style2 = ?red, "Combining styles");
    let combined = bold.combine(&red);
    debug!(result = ?combined, "Combined style");
    
    assert!(combined.attributes.is_set(Attributes::BOLD));
    info!("Style combination PASSED");
}
```","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","owner":"jeff141421@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-01-17T10:23:45.709080256-05:00","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone","updated_at":"2026-01-17T10:45:59.866293688-05:00","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"rich_rust-8cf","depends_on_id":"rich_rust-33q","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-17T10:24:24.680310869-05:00","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone"}]}
 {"id":"rich_rust-8ut","title":"Implement cell_len Caching for Performance","description":"## Objective
Add caching to cell_len() calculations for improved performance.

## Background
cell_len() calculates the display width of strings in terminal cells. It's called extensively during text wrapping, table layout, and alignment. Caching can significantly improve performance.

## Technical Details

### Current State
- cell_len() iterates over every character, calling get_character_cell_size()
- No caching of computed widths
- Repeated calls for same strings recompute from scratch

### Proposed Implementation
Two-level caching:
1. Character-level: cache width of individual chars (probably already via unicode-width)
2. String-level: cache total width of frequently-used strings

```rust
use std::sync::Mutex;
use lru::LruCache;

lazy_static! {
    static ref WIDTH_CACHE: Mutex<LruCache<String, usize>> = 
        Mutex::new(LruCache::new(1024));
}

pub fn cell_len_cached(s: &str) -> usize {
    // Short strings: compute directly (cache overhead not worth it)
    if s.len() < 8 {
        return cell_len(s);
    }
    
    let mut cache = WIDTH_CACHE.lock().unwrap();
    if let Some(&width) = cache.get(s) {
        return width;
    }
    let width = cell_len(s);
    cache.put(s.to_string(), width);
    width
}
```

### Considerations
- Cache invalidation: not needed (width is deterministic)
- Memory: bound cache size to prevent growth
- Short string threshold: benchmark to find optimal cutoff
- Thread safety: use appropriate synchronization

## Files to Modify
- src/cells.rs (add caching)
- Cargo.toml (lru dependency if not already added)

## Testing
- Benchmark table rendering with/without cache
- Test with various text sizes
- Verify thread safety","acceptance_criteria":"cell_len caching implemented. Benchmarks show measurable improvement.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","owner":"jeff141421@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-01-17T10:21:13.116410969-05:00","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone","updated_at":"2026-01-17T10:21:13.116410969-05:00","labels":["implementation","performance"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"rich_rust-8ut","depends_on_id":"rich_rust-a96","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-17T10:22:55.897283402-05:00","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone"},{"issue_id":"rich_rust-8ut","depends_on_id":"rich_rust-yz6","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-17T10:25:08.598353178-05:00","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone"},{"issue_id":"rich_rust-8ut","depends_on_id":"rich_rust-cwe","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-17T10:43:23.076534202-05:00","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone"}]}
